idea: Build RouteGuard, a local field-service sequence planner using a fictional travel-time matrix. It simulates one delay, propagates downstream ETAs and SLA risk, and lets schedulers reorder visits accessibly.
audience: Regional field-service schedulers managing a technician's daily sequence
outcome: Schedulers can understand cascading delay risk and improve the visit order while preserving an auditable deterministic calculation.
constraints: No maps, backend, authentication, database, analytics, APIs, or runtime network calls
Use a documented fictional deterministic travel-time matrix
Reordering must be keyboard accessible and support undo plus reset
State cannot rely on color alone and must work on narrow screens
must_have_capabilities: Show ordered jobs, cumulative ETAs, SLA deadlines, and current risk
Apply one deterministic delay simulation and propagate downstream ETAs
Update ETA and SLA-risk counts after reordering
Undo the reorder and reset the scenario
visual_direction: A sequence-first scheduling console with readable time math, clear before-and-after risk, and no map dependency.
demo_preferences: Simulate a delay, show downstream SLA risks, reorder one visit, show ETAs and risk count improving, undo, then reset.
